key_insight: MACE-sqs potentials more accurately describe the magnetic and structural properties of Fe-Ni alloys than previous models.
engineering_relevance: Accurate modeling of Fe-Ni alloys is crucial for applications in materials engineering and nanotechnology.
method: Systematic benchmark of MACE potentials against spin-polarized PBE DFT calculations.
Evaluation of structural, elastic, magnetic, and temperature-dependent properties.
Training a system-specific MACE-sqs model on a diverse reference dataset.
evidence_signal: MACE-sqs achieves validation errors of 2.0 meV/atom for energies and 24.3 meV/Angstrom for forces.
watch_item: High-pressure magnetic collapse and the effect of composition on transition pressure are not fully captured.
paper_title: Can MACE Potentials Accurately Describe Magnetism and Phase Stability in Fe-Ni Alloys? A Systematic Benchmark
